0.response: {"Q0":"The only strategy I had was in relation to the tools used to dig, the saw, shovel, and drill. I tried to memorize the letter, I would say J is soil, K is wood, and L is rock. So I don't have to keep looking at my keys."}

7.response: {"Q0":"I think mainly the percentage of the outcome and in relation to the price, I wasn't going to choose the Galaxy with a low outcome, but I took my chances if it was a high value amount."}

5.response: {"Q0":"Yes I did keep digging. I should have stopped, as it wasn't going to add to my total gold, since the astronaut had left. But I think I did stop digging after."}

2.response: {"Q0":"I feel like I didn't find it as much as I thought, there were green ice outcomes towards the end, but I got mostly normal rocks I feel."}

6.response: {"Q0":"Yes, I stuck to collecting only the blue and red gems. I think I still did collect blue gems after the astrounaut had left."}

8.response: {"Q0":"I was mainly focused on visiting the planets and digging to find the gems. I thought of that was the \"main\" part of the game, as I was trying to get as much as gold I can."}

3.response: {"Q0":"Yes for example the blue astronaut left, and it referred to the blue crystal type on the blue planet."}

11.response: {"Q0":"I don't have any constructive feedback, but overall I really liked the concept of the game."}
